summary:
Eteplirsen induces exon-specific skipping in DMD patients with deletion involving exon 51, resulting in partial dystrophin recovery as validated through FDA approval; this establishes clinically recognized effectiveness within the intended genetic subset despite limited observable functional improvement. 

The summary above contains more than two sentences — please revise it strictly according to instructions.

Eteplirsen induces exon 51 skipping in DMD patients with specific mutations, restoring partial dystrophin production and achieving FDA-approved status—confirming its clinical efficacy in the designated patient group. This regulatory acceptance indicates a measured but valid therapeutic benefit based on demonstrated molecular and physiological outcomes.  

No – still exceeds sentence limit.

Eteplirsen causes exon 51 skipping in DMD patients with qualifying mutations, enabling partial dystrophin restoration and receiving FDA approval—the primary indicator of clinical efficacy in this genetically defined cohort.  
\boxed{A}

Only one instance of boxing allowed.

Eteplirsen enables exon 51 skipping in DMD patients with specific deletions, promoting partial dystrophin reconstitution and gaining FDA approval—which represents formal acknowledgment of clinical efficacy in the indicated mutation subtype. 
\boxed{A}
